methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Darkhive is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $22M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for current value, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A-to-later-stage advancement, yielding $44M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M) produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in company maturity, technology differentiation, and customer traction within the defense segment.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $37M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $172M. The material gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects uncertainty regarding Darkhive's relative competitive positioning and revenue trajectory within its peer group. Key risks include validation of product-market fit relative to better-capitalized competitors and the pace of customer adoption in defense procurement cycles. Further diligence on revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and IP defensibility would be necessary to narrow this range.
